name: Fantasy Fireball VFX
description: A magical fireball effect: a glowing core, layered particle emission, heat distortion, additive bloom, and a trailing motion path. Targets Three.js / R3F shader-based particle systems with post-processing bloom.

requiredElements.name: glowing-core
requiredElements.description: A bright emissive sphere mesh at the fireball center with a high-intensity emissive material (color temperature ~1800K-2200K orange).

requiredElements.name: particle-emitter
requiredElements.description: A GPU-friendly Points or instanced mesh system emitting sparks that drift outward and fade; particle count tunable, frustum-culled.

requiredElements.name: additive-blending
requiredElements.description: Core and particle materials use AdditiveBlending so overlapping glow accumulates into white-hot regions.

requiredElements.name: motion-trail
requiredElements.description: A trailing path behind the fireball (ribbon geometry or fading instanced quads) that follows the projectile's trajectory.

requiredElements.name: bloom-postprocess
requiredElements.description: An UnrealBloomPass (or equivalent) in the post-processing chain with threshold tuned so only the core/highlights bloom.

optionalElements.name: heat-distortion
optionalElements.description: A subtle refraction/distortion shader layer around the core simulating heat shimmer.

optionalElements.name: smoke-trail
optionalElements.description: A secondary darker, slower-fading smoke particle system behind the sparks.

optionalElements.name: point-light
optionalElements.description: A dynamic PointLight parented to the core to cast real light on nearby geometry.

optionalElements.name: launch-flash
optionalElements.description: A one-shot expanding ring flash at the spawn point that fades over ~200ms.

qualityRules.description: Core emissive intensity high enough to read as a light source, not a flat sprite.
Color transitions from white-yellow core to orange-red edges (temperature gradient).
Fireball scale reads as a projectile, not a static sprite; has perceived velocity.
Particles fade opacity over lifetime, not pop in/out.
Trail smoothly follows the core with no jagged disconnects.
Bloom threshold set so only emissive highlights bloom, not the whole scene.
Particle geometry/material/disposables are cleaned up when the effect ends (no leaks).
Particle count bounded (<=2000) and uses BufferGeometry attributes, not per-frame allocations.
Effect is frustum-culled / paused when off-screen.

antiPatterns.description: Using a single flat sprite texture for the core with no emissive 3D volume reads as a decal, not a fireball.
A single uniform orange color with no temperature gradient looks cheap and unmagical.
Particles that instantly appear/disappear without opacity fade break immersion.
Bloom threshold too low washes out the entire scene; only highlights should bloom.
Creating new BufferGeometry/Vector3 in the render loop causes GC stutter.

improvementSuggestions.description: Add a heat-distortion shader layer for a richer magical feel.
Add a spawn-point flash ring to sell the ignition moment.
Parent a PointLight to the core so it illuminates the environment.

exampleOutputs.label: R3F fireball core + emitter
exampleOutputs.snippet:
<points ref={ref} frustumCulled={false}>
  <bufferGeometry>
    <bufferAttribute attach="attributes-position" args={[positions, 3]} />
    <bufferAttribute attach="attributes-alpha" args={[alphas, 1]} />
  </bufferGeometry>
  <shaderMaterial
    blending={THREE.AdditiveBlending}
    transparent
    depthWrite={false}
    vertexShader={vert}
    fragmentShader={frag}
  />
</points>
